novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025 §4 requires the two interval inequalities pointwise for its bounds; its G(t)>=0 procedure is explicitly a practical expectation proxy, so calling average overlap the paper's exact compatibility rule is inaccurate.

soundness_flags.label: def:two-stratum-witness / Proposition 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The construction specifies X,S,A probabilities and conditional means but no conditional law for Y, so it does not define the asserted probability law P^{can}; specify, for example, Y|(X,S,A)~nu_{mu_{SA}(X)}.

soundness_flags.label: ass:sieve-growth, ass:score-complexity, and def:uniform-class
soundness_flags.one_line: The purported class of laws is carved partly by a tuning sequence and an undefined active-score class, rather than primitive properties of P; move learner/bootstrap conditions to the inference construction and define the score class explicitly.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Reframe Theorem 1 against Lanners et al. precisely: their population theory retains pointwise feasibility, while the proposed advance is a computable exact latent-extension characterization and a correction to their signed-average operational diagnostic.
Complete P^{can} by giving every observed conditional outcome kernel, then retain the displayed arithmetic as an explicit nondegenerate cancellation exhibit.
Define the active-face scores, basis, bootstrap critical value, and random-set inversion; state primitive nuisance regularity separately from algorithm-specific entropy and rate conditions.

recommended_next_step: Revise the comparator characterization and complete the witness and inference-class definitions; the repaired exact-feasibility kernel remains plausibly field-tier.
